title: Austin Unsupervised Robotaxi Fleet Reaches 10 Model Ys
summary: Tesla's Austin Robotaxi fleet hit a record of 10 unsupervised Model Y vehicles operating driverlessly.
impact_reason: Increasing Austin's unsupervised fleet to 10 vehicles represents mild progress in core hub scaling, providing more data for software improvements and potential expansion.

title: Tesla's New Dallas and Houston Robotaxi Fleets Reach Four Vehicles Total
summary: After launch, Tesla added vehicles in Dallas and Houston, reaching two unsupervised vehicles per city; Austin shows 13 unsupervised vehicles active recently. This supports Tesla's H1 2026 plan for seven-city expansion.
impact_reason: Early fleet buildup in new cities to four total vehicles indicates initial scaling momentum spilling over from Austin, modestly boosting overall unsupervised operations velocity.

title: Tesla Expands Unsupervised Robotaxi to Dallas and Houston
summary: Tesla announced the rollout of unsupervised Robotaxi service in Dallas and Houston, Texas, with initial geofence areas defined and video evidence showing no safety drivers. This triples Tesla's active Robotaxi hubs in Texas, building on ongoing operations in Austin.
impact_reason: Tripling the Texas Robotaxi footprint with two new unsupervised cities directly accelerates overall fleet scaling velocity by expanding operational areas and data collection for faster growth beyond Austin.

title: Tesla Discloses 14 Crashes in Austin Robotaxi Operations
summary: In a February filing, Tesla reported 14 crashes involving its Austin robotaxis since launch.
impact_reason: Disclosed crashes introduce mild safety scrutiny that could slightly slow regulatory approvals or public acceptance, indirectly dampening unsupervised fleet scaling velocity in Austin.
